Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (sports betting)

Provision.Text: This enactment repeals paragraph 207(4)(b) of the Criminal Code to make it lawful for the government of a province, or a person or entity licensed by the Lieutenant Governor in Council of that province, to conduct and manage a lottery scheme in the province that involves betting on a race or fight or on a single sport event or athletic contest.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Safe and Regulated Sports Betting Act.
Subsection 207(4) of the Criminal Code is amended by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (a) and by repealing paragraph (b).
This Act comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
